title: Issue 2311 review worktree isolation

retention.reason: completed-plan cleanup retained compact closeout evidence instead of a full execplan archive

outcome: Closed stale #2311 after source ancestry and focused proof verification.
evidence_refs: implementation ancestry: a9d44625 isolate review resumes in disposable worktrees
test_resume_reuses_owned_worktree_and_updates_it_to_recorded_handoff: exact-session reuse updates the owned checkout to the recorded handoff head without creating a second worktree
test_fresh_global_dispatch_fetches_and_detaches_at_reviewed_head: blocked review dispatch fetches the reviewed head and creates a detached disposable worktree
test_detached_fresh_stop_hook_binds_precreated_owner_state: Stop handoff binds a detached fresh session to precreated owner state

test_interrupted_fresh_dispatch_is_recovered_once_then_resumes_the_recorded_session: interrupted fresh owner state is replaced once, the stale worktree is removed, and resume uses the recorded session
test_global_dispatch_retains_one_owned_worktree_through_resume_then_retires_it_on_close: lifecycle proof covers fresh handoff, resume handoff, push-facing head update, and cleanup on terminal close
test_explicit_worktree_replacement_retires_owned_checkout_and_state: explicit recovery cleanup removes the owned checkout and dispatch state

test_orphaned_worktree_failure_gets_one_automatic_recovery and test_resume_failure_gets_one_automatic_recovery_for_same_comment: creation and resume failures are bounded to one automatic recovery for the same review

closure.provenance: completed-plan cleanup retained compact closeout evidence instead of a full execplan archive

proof_report.proof boundary: Exact-session reuse, detached worktree isolation from a foreign owner branch, owner-state handoff routing, push/cleanup success, and bounded creation/cleanup failure behavior are covered by the cited focused tests and implementation ancestry commit a9d44625.

closure_check.reopen trigger: None unless new evidence shows the closeout was incomplete.
